## Methods

<!-- Nature Letter: Methods are placed AFTER the main text and
     references (before Extended Data). For Frontiers in Biogeography,
     Methods come before Results.

     Suggested sub-sections:
     - Study region and temporal scope
     - Vegetation data (VegVault, fossil pollen archives, gridpoints)
     - Abiotic predictors (bioclimatic variables)
     - Taxonomic harmonisation
     - Community data preparation
     - Joint species distribution modelling (HMSC)
     - Model evaluation
     - Statistical analyses (ANOVA decomposition, etc.)
-->

```{r}
#| label: methods-data-summary
#| echo: false
#| output: false
# Load any targets needed to report sample sizes, date ranges, etc.
# Example:
# data_community <- targets::tar_read(
#   name = "data_community",
#   store = set_store
# )
```

### Study region and temporal scope

TODO: Describe geographic extent and time period covered.

### Vegetation and abiotic data

TODO: Describe VegVault data extraction, fossil pollen archives,
modern vegetation gridpoints, and bioclimatic predictors.

### Data processing

TODO: Describe taxonomic harmonisation, filtering steps, and
community matrix construction.

### Joint species distribution modelling

TODO: Describe HMSC model structure, random effects, priors,
MCMC settings, and convergence diagnostics.

### Model evaluation and inference

TODO: Describe predictive performance metrics and co-occurrence
extraction approach.
